sourceMovie.title: Nerve
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Nerve (2016) stuck because it commits to its mystery premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Henry Joost's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The mystery and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Losers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Nerve clicked for you, The Losers (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in mystery and adventure, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Kick-Ass 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Nerve clicked for you, Kick-Ass 2 (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Nerve?
faq.answer: Try The Losers, Kick-Ass 2, Paycheck—each shares DNA with Nerve while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Nerve?
faq.answer: Nerve (2016) is best described as Mystery, Adventure, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Nerve worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this mystery lane.
